mnemonics: NEURONS: Nodes Emitting Unipolar Reactions, Organizing Nervous Signals. This helps recall that biological neurons are the fundamental units that process and transmit electrochemical signals in the nervous system.
keyPoints: A biological neuron is a specialized cell that receives, processes, and transmits information through electrical and chemical signals.
Key components include the dendrites (input receivers), cell body (integrator), axon (signal transmitter), and axon terminals (output transmitters via synapses).
Neurons communicate via action potentials (all-or-nothing electrical spikes) and neurotransmitters (chemical messengers released at synapses).
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of axons and dendrites (input vs. output).
Thinking a neuron's output is a graded signal rather than a discrete, all-or-nothing action potential.
Over-simplifying the comparison to an artificial neuron in a neural network, ignoring the biological complexity of chemical synapses and refractory periods.
